entries.title: Man kills seven of his children, and an eighth child, in Louisiana mass shooting
entries.snippet: Three boys and five girls, aged between three to 11, were killed in the shooting on Sunday, officials say.

entries.title: Oil prices rise after Trump says Iranian ship seized
entries.snippet: Energy markets have seen wild swings since the US and Israel attacked Iran on 28 February.

entries.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
entries.snippet: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

entries.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers allegedly fled deadly shooting
entries.snippet: Authorities say the officers have been suspended and an investigation into their actions is under way.

entries.title: New Zealand declares state of emergency in Wellington as floods hit
entries.snippet: Footage online shows vehicles submerged, trees uprooted and houses hit by landslides.

entries.title: Rumen Radev's party wins Bulgarian election
entries.snippet: With 87% of the vote now counted, Progressive Bulgaria has secured a majority in parliament.

entries.title: Iranian woman arrested in US for allegedly trafficking arms to Sudan
entries.snippet: Shamim Mafi is accused of brokering the sales of arms to Sudan's defence ministry on behalf of Iran.

entries.title: Rebel Wilson's claims against actress are 'malicious concoctions', Australian court hears
entries.snippet: Wilson has been accused of defaming the star of her film The Deb over an incident involving a bath in Bondi.

entries.title: At least 25 killed in firecracker factory blast in India
entries.snippet: Mostly female workers have been killed in the blast, which occurred in Tamil Nadu state.

entries.title: Thousands of Parisians evacuated as WW2 bomb detonated
entries.snippet: Residents within a 450m radius of the device were told to leave their homes while it was neutralised.

entries.title: Rat poison found in HiPP baby food jar in Austria, police say
entries.snippet: The brand warned on Saturday that consuming the purées may be potentially "life-threatening".

entries.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford agrees to sell $21m private jet following outcry
entries.snippet: After confirming the purchase on Friday, Ford drew critcism from political opponents who called it a "gravy plane" and "out of touch".

entries.title: Zelensky condemns US extension of Russian sanctions waiver
entries.snippet: The US argues that the waiver is meant to ease the energy supply crunch sparked by the US-Israel war with Iran.

entries.title: Pope says 'tyrants' speech was not aimed at Trump
entries.snippet: The pontiff says his remarks have been misinterpreted after a spat with the US president.

entries.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
entries.snippet: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

entries.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
entries.snippet: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

entries.title: India has splurged billions on metro trains. But where are the commuters?
entries.snippet: Without better last-mile connectivity and affordable fares, metro use is unlikely to improve quickly, say experts.

entries.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries have responded to rising oil prices
entries.snippet: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

entries.title: Art on trial - a sculptor's arrest highlights new extremes for censorship in China
entries.snippet: Gao Zhen is being retroactively punished for 15 year-old-works, in a case that has alarmed rights groups.

entries.title: Why your recycled clothes could end up in this South American desert
entries.snippet: Old garments from around the world are being discarded in the South American country.

entries.title: Peace talks in doubt as U.S. seizes Iranian ship
entries.snippet: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

entries.title: A mine despoiled the beauty of the rainforest. This Goldman Prize winner took action
entries.snippet: "We women are the land guardians and keepers," says Theonila Roka Matbob of Papua New Guinea, recognized for her efforts to repair the environmental and social harms caused by a copper and gold mine.

entries.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
entries.snippet: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

entries.title: Voters say they feel confused and misled on Virginia's redistricting vote
entries.snippet: Contradictory election mailers, conflicting TV ads and vague wording on the ballot have Virginia voters saying that the campaigns on either side of the redistricting vote are muddying the waters.

entries.title: The Sonoran Desert teems with wildlife. These 3D scans could help protect its future
entries.snippet: A new art exhibit in Phoenix features some of the world's prickliest plants. It could also help save them.

entries.title: Who owns presidential records? Trump's Justice Department says it's him
entries.snippet: The Trump administration asserts that a nearly 50-year-old law requiring the preservation of presidential records is unconstitutional. Historians warn important papers could be destroyed.

entries.title: Spanish speakers learn strategies to pass English-only driving test in Florida
entries.snippet: A new English-only driving test rule in Florida is fueling a surge in strategy lessons for Spanish speakers where they learn to figure out the questions without having to take English language courses.

entries.title: Got wearable data? Your doctor can help you connect the dots
entries.snippet: The American Academy of Neurology issued guidance on using wearable data devices, like smartwatches or an Oura Ring, to track key health metrics that can help flag serious conditions.

entries.title: Data centers are expensive, unpopular — and could be a tipping point in the midterms
entries.snippet: Anger over the data center boom has spilled into politics with voters unseating local politicians who support them. It's become an issue hard to ignore in the midterm elections.

entries.title: Morning news brief
entries.snippet: Trump announces planned Iran war peace talks, Tehran signals it may boycott negotiations amid ongoing U.S. naval pressure, businesses can now apply for Trump tariff refunds.

entries.title: Oil prices spike again following latest standoff in the Strait of Hormuz
entries.snippet: The ongoing conflict with the U.S. and Iran prevented tankers from passing through. The price of U.S. crude oil increased 6.4% to $87.88 per barrel after trading resumed on the Chicago Mercantile Exchange. The price of Brent crude, the international standard, climbed 6.5% to $96.

entries.title: Los Angeles woman arrested on suspicion of helping Iran traffic weapons to Sudan
entries.snippet: First U.S. Attorney Bill Essayli said Sunday on social media that the woman will face charges that she brokered the sale of "drones, bombs, bomb fuses, and millions of rounds of ammunition" between Iran and the Sudanese Armed Forces.

entries.title: Canada's prime minister says economic connection to the U.S. has shifted from a strength to a weakness
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Mark Carney said tariffs imposed by U.S. President Donald Trump have impacted workers in such major industries as auto and steel and prompted businesses to hold back investments.

entries.title: Chernobyl full of life as wildlife reoccupies a radioactive landscape
entries.snippet: Wolves, bears and lynx have rebounded in the radioactive landscape, along with a rare breed of horses native to Mongolia. Scientists say it shows nature's ability to recover when human activity is removed.

entries.title: U.S. invests in project to remove rare earth minerals despite differences with South Africa
entries.snippet: The U.S. has committed a $50 million investment in the Phalaborwa Rare Earths Project despite U.S.-South Africa relations plummeting to their lowest point in decades.

entries.title: Police investigating arson attacks at Jewish sites in London
entries.snippet: British police say they are looking at whether the incidents are the work of Iranian proxies.

entries.title: Former president Rumen Radev will win Bulgaria election, exit poll suggests
entries.snippet: SOFIA, Bulgaria (AP) — An exit poll in Bulgaria suggested Sunday that the center-left Progressive Bulgaria coalition led by ex-president Rumen Radev will be the winner of the country's parliamentary election. The poll conducted by Trend research group showed Radev's coalition ear

entries.title: 5 people shot during fight in nightlife district near the University of Iowa, police say
entries.snippet: Authorities in Iowa say three University of Iowa students have been wounded in a shooting that erupted in a nightlife district near campus. Two others were injured in the shooting that happened early Sunday in downtown Iowa City.

entries.title: Russian strikes kill at least 2 as Ukraine targets drone factory inside Russia
entries.snippet: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian strikes killed at least two people in Ukraine, officials said Sunday, as the Ukrainian military struck a drone factory in southwestern Russia. A "massive" nighttime drone strike on Chernihiv in northern Ukraine killed a 16-year-old boy and wounded fou

entries.title: Gunman kills 8 children in Louisiana mass shooting
entries.snippet: Officials say a gunman in Louisiana killed eight children, including seven of his own, and shot two other people Sunday. Police say the victims ranged in age from 1 to about 12 years old and one of the adults wounded was the mother of the suspect's children.

entries.title: Humanoid robot wins Beijing half-marathon, defeating the human world record
entries.snippet: This year's race featured over 300 robots from more than 100 teams, including international participants. The competition highlights China's advancements in a technological competition with the U.S.

entries.title: Eddie Murphy receives AFI life achievement award along with star-studded tribute
entries.snippet: The American Film Institute ceremony, held Saturday night at the Dolby Theatre, featured appearances from Spike Lee, Martin Lawrence, Dave Chappelle, and many more.

entries.title: FBI and Justice Department try to rebuild after wave of resignations and firings
entries.snippet: The agencies leaders are easing hiring requirements and accelerating recruitment in ways that some current and former officials see as a lowering of long-accepted standards.

entries.title: North Korea tests short-range ballistic missiles
entries.snippet: South Korea's military was analyzing whether the latest launches were made from a submarine, a land-based launcher or both platforms, according to South Korean media.

entries.title: U.S. attacks Iranian-flagged ship as Tehran promises to respond
entries.snippet: It was the first interception since the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports began last week. Iran's joint military command called the armed boarding an act of piracy and a ceasefire violation, the state broadcaster said.

entries.title: How consumers can navigate the cost of available flights impacted by the Iran war
entries.snippet: Experts say booking earlier, and staying flexible on dates, destinations and departure airports can help reduce costs.

entries.title: Obama and Mamdani meet for the first time
entries.snippet: The meeting comes as the New York mayor, a democratic socialist, is also trying to build a working relationship with Republican President Donald Trump. The meeting comes just over a week after Mamdani marked his 100th day in office.

entries.title: Witnesses detail deadly Israeli strikes on medics in Lebanon
entries.snippet: The three strikes, which killed four paramedics and wounded six others, is the latest example of Israel's willingness to target Lebanon's health sector. The attacks on the ambulances have drawn condemnation, including from the United Nations' human rights office.

entries.title: Arizona midterms begin with spotlight on largest county's controversial election system
entries.snippet: Much of the political spotlight this election year in Arizona has been focused on the office that runs elections in Maricopa County, the state's largest. Republican Justin Heap is overseeing elections in the Phoenix-area and he's an election skeptic.

entries.title: How to spot the Lyrid meteor shower that is peaking this week
entries.snippet: The show will be visible across the globe, but views will be best in the Northern Hemisphere. And there's no risk of the crescent moon photobombing the Lyrid shower. It'll set before the fun starts.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Is To Lam's Vietnam turning into Xi Jinping's China?
entries.snippet: After Vietnam's lawmakers unanimously elected Communist Party chief To Lam as the country's president, analysts are asking — is Vietnam trying to copy China's political model?

entries.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
entries.snippet: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

entries.title: South Korea, India agree to boost trade ties amid global tensions
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung this week as both countries target $50 billion in trade by 2030.

entries.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
entries.snippet: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

entries.title: How China is reshaping the global chip industry
entries.snippet: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

entries.title: Japan issues tsunami alert after strong earthquake
entries.snippet: The 7.4-magnitude quake off northern Iwate prefecture could be felt as far as Tokyo. Residents have been urged to evacuate northern coastal regions ahead of a tsunami.

entries.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev on course to win election
entries.snippet: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria, is on course to win Bulgaria's eighth general election in five years. But Radev's pro-Moscow tilt puts pressure on Brussels.

entries.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
entries.snippet: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot. DW has the latest.

entries.title: Iran war: 'No plans' for fresh peace talks, Tehran says
entries.snippet: Pakistan is preparing to host a second round of negotiations, but doubts remain as to whether they will take place, after the US seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. DW has the latest.

entries.title: US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3
entries.snippet: Trump has said that the US is in "armed conflict" with cartels, as its military campaign has killed at least 180 people since last year amid criticism over its legality.

entries.title: Germany news: Timmy the stranded whale back on the move
entries.snippet: The struggling humpback whale is due to be fitted with a radio transmitter to help follow his recovery. Meanwhile, Chancellor Merz has promised that Germany is prepared for any crisis. DW has more.

entries.title: Venezuela's oil reform aims to lure private investors
entries.snippet: After the US ousted Maduro and effectively seized control of Venezuela's oil industry, the country's economy has been transitioning rapidly. The interim government is wooing investors but lacks democratic backing.

entries.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
entries.snippet: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

entries.title: Canada's US ties have become a weakness, says Carney
entries.snippet: Canadian PM Mark Carney has vowed to reduce his country's reliance on the US by strengthening the economy and inking trade deals with others.

entries.title: Iran war: Tehran vows response to US seizure of cargo ship
entries.snippet: US Marines have seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship near the Hormuz Strait, President Trump has said. Earlier, he threatened to destroy every bridge and power plant in Iran if no peace deal was reached.

entries.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
entries.snippet: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

entries.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
entries.snippet: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

entries.title: US mass shooting leaves 8 children dead in Louisiana
entries.snippet: A "domestic disturbance" in the US state of Louisiana has claimed the lives of eight children, including a 1-year-old, police have said.

entries.title: Mines in the Strait of Hormuz: How dangerous are they?
entries.snippet: Iran says it has placed mines in the Straight of Hormuz to discourage independent traffic. How dangerous are naval mines, and what can be done to clear them?
